error.signature: MongoServerError: BSONObjectTooLarge: object to insert exceeds maximum BSON document size (16793600 > 16777216)

verdict.summary: Document exceeds MongoDB's 16MB BSON size limit. Usually caused by unbounded arrays growing over time.

dead_ends.action: Increase the BSON document size limit
dead_ends.why_fails: The 16MB limit is a hard server-side constraint compiled into MongoDB. It cannot be changed via configuration.
BSON size is measured after serialization. Application-level compression does not bypass the BSON size limit check.

workarounds.action: Refactor unbounded arrays into separate collection with references
workarounds.how: Instead of: { comments: [...10000] }, use comments collection: { postId: ObjectId, text: '...' }

workarounds.action: Use GridFS for large binary data
workarounds.how: const bucket = new GridFSBucket(db); bucket.openUploadStream('largefile.bin')  # chunks data across documents

workarounds.action: Use the bucket pattern to limit array growth
workarounds.how: Split into buckets: { postId: 1, bucket: 0, comments: [...200] }, { postId: 1, bucket: 1, comments: [...200] }
